Explanation: This technical report presents a comprehensive quality assessment of HPLC chromatography data with strong methodological rigor. The three-stage pipeline (sanitization, signal validation, outlier classification) is well-structured and addresses real data ingestion challenges. The analysis demonstrates solid scientific fundamentals with appropriate use of statistical metrics (CV, MAPE, Gaussian fitting) and regulatory compliance validation (USP §621‹). The dual-path outlier detection methodology effectively distinguishes between artifacts and process anomalies. Visualization is effective with grid layouts showing raw vs. smoothed signals and annotated metrics. Key strengths include quantitative results (190,855 outliers classified; column-specific CVs) and actionable insights. Minor areas for improvement: some sections are dense; the writing occasionally uses unexplained symbols (¡); and future work recommendations could be more specific. The code appendix demonstrates implementation rigor but adds significant length. Overall, this is a well-executed analysis suitable for data validation in regulated environments.
